input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Check if .NET is already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (discovers latest stable)
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8 --accept-source-agreements

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (pulls Android SDK, build tools, emulator, etc.)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows target needs the Windows App SDK and WinUI tooling. Easiest via VS:

```powershell
# Install VS 2022 Community with required workloads
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Then add the required workload components
# (Run from an elevated prompt)
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --includeRecommended --quiet --wait
```

## 4. Set Up Android SDK & Emulator

The `dotnet workload install maui` installs Android SDK components under `$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk`. If you need to manage them:

```powershell
# Discover where the Android SDK landed
$androidSdk = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Microsoft\Android\sdk"
$sdkManager = Join-Path $androidSdk "cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"

# List installed packages
& $sdkManager --list_installed

# List all available packages (discover versions dynamically)
& $sdkManager --list | Select-String "platforms;android-" | Sort-Object
& $sdkManager --list | Select-String "build-tools;" | Sort-Object
& $sdkManager --list | Select-String "system-images.*google_apis" | Sort-Object

# Install specific components (adjust versions from above output)
& $sdkManager "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0" `
  "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

# Accept all licenses
& $sdkManager --licenses
```

## 5. Create an Android Emulator

```powershell
$avdManager = Join-Path $androidSdk "cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat"

# List available device definitions
& $avdManager list device | Select-String "Name:"

# Create an AVD (pick a device name from above)
& $avdManager create avd -n "Pixel_5_API34" `
  -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" `
  -d "pixel_5" --force

# Verify
& $avdManager list avd
```

## 6. Enable Windows Developer Mode

Required for deploying UWP/WinUI apps:

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode via registry (requires elevation)
reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1
```

## 7. Set Environment Variables

```powershell
# Persist ANDROID_HOME so tools can find the SDK
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME",
  (Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Microsoft\Android\sdk"), "User")

# Add platform-tools (adb) to PATH
$platformTools = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Microsoft\Android\sdk\platform-tools"
$currentPath = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "User")
if ($currentPath -notlike "*$platformTools*") {
  [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("Path",
    "$currentPath;$platformTools", "User")
}
```

## 8. Create & Run a Test Project

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
$emulator = Join-Path $env:ANDROID_HOME "emulator\emulator.exe"
Start-Process $emulator -ArgumentList "-avd Pixel_5_API34"
dotnet run -f net8.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0
```

## 9. Verify Everything

```powershell
# One-shot health check
dotnet workload list
dotnet --list-sdks
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed
adb devices   # should show emulator if running
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `net8.0-*` with `net9.0-*` if you install .NET 9 SDK instead.
- Hyper-V or WHPX must be enabled for Android emulator acceleration (`Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All`).
- All version discovery is built into the commands above (`--list`, `workload search`, `winget search`).
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands now, so they want all the PowerShell commands ready to go. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
